His father was a harsh man, given to moods and fits of temper that he vented on his children.
Walt Disney was a master of condensation. Strip everything away and get to the essence.
Walt never wanted to repeat himself. He was always interested in what he hadn't done.
Mediocrity was Walt’s affliction, the thing he detested most.
Walt Disney was never interested in creating animated cartoons. He wanted to create new and special kinds of entertainment.

title: Walt Disney: An American Original by Bob Thomas | Goodreads
excerpts:
Section Title: Walt Disney: An American Original > Ratings & Reviews > Community Reviews
Content:
Walt's no-repeats mantra (coined after film distributors requested that he make a sequel to the 3 Little Pigs short), "You can't top pigs with pigs," kept appearing at moments like these. The possibility of easy money to be made on the tried-and-true had no allure for him--he wanted always to find the next big challenge (such a far cry from what the company has moved to these days, in acquiring pre-existing franchises and sequelling its films into direct-to-video hell!). That pioneering spirit is what set him apart. A highly enjoyable book about a very unique individual.
